question: What does Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) do according to the notes?

options.text: Retrieves external context at query time without retraining the base model.
options.feedback: Correct: RAG injects retrieved knowledge dynamically during answer generation.

options.text: Retrains the language model on every user query for personalization.
options.feedback: Per-query retraining is impractical; RAG uses retrieval, not model updates.

options.text: Disables retrieval and relies only on parametric model memory.
options.feedback: That is the opposite of RAG, which explicitly depends on retrieval.

options.text: Compresses vector indexes into static prompts before deployment.
options.feedback: RAG performs runtime retrieval rather than one-time prompt compression.

whyQuestion: Why is RAG useful for personalized or current answers?

whyOptions.text: Because it can inject relevant private or recent data during inference.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: retrieval supplies fresh context that base training may not include.

whyOptions.text: Because it guarantees factual accuracy without retrieval quality concerns.
whyOptions.feedback: Accuracy still depends on retrieval relevance and source reliability.

whyOptions.text: Because it permanently writes fetched documents into model weights.
whyOptions.feedback: RAG context is transient and does not alter core model parameters.

whyOptions.text: Because it removes token limits by bypassing context windows entirely.
whyOptions.feedback: RAG helps relevance, but generated prompts still respect context limits.
